subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Future of Space Exploration
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Future of Space Exploration.

hook.text: Artemis aims to return humans to the Moon. SpaceX plans to send people to Mars. The next era of exploration has begun.

buildup.text: Reusable rockets from SpaceX and Blue Origin have slashed launch costs, opening space to more missions than ever.

discovery.text: Private companies are building space stations, lunar landers, and even asteroid-mining spacecraft.

twist.text: China has its own space station and is planning crewed lunar missions. A new space race is underway.

climax.text: Within decades, humans may live on the Moon and Mars. The species that gazed at stars may finally reach them.

punchline.text: From Stonehenge to starships. The sky was always the beginning, not the limit.

quiz.question: What has made the current era of space exploration different from the original Space Race?
options: Private companies have dramatically reduced launch costs with reusable rockets
Government funding for space has increased tenfold
New propulsion systems allow faster-than-light travel
